pullRequest.title: QE-15074 Qe 15074 add firefox specific workflow

nodes.body: are you saying to have all the ff tests in this separate folder? Just wondering since it makes tests more separated by type rather than "Feature".
all of the firefox-only tests would be in this folder yes.
I added the ability to tag though, idk what the best design choice to trigger this though

I think you may need a shorter prefix....
```suggestion
      deployment_prefix: run-ff-only
```
I don't think making it ff is a great idea, I thought about this too, but this conflicts with the "Feature Flag" terminology.

sorry, let's not add yet another param, but key off the test type instead
```suggestion
```
Why the five second sleep? And I think it shouldn't be a numeral 5, but rather a well named argument in cucurc.

I believe it is a time used to wait for the window to finish its render after the change in window size. Will say that this is a branch that was merged from @ddl-galias 's work for setting up the test for firefox. I believe I took a cursory glance at this at CKO. So, I may need to get in touch with him on this choice.
I also don't like the sleep. There is no guarantee that the time is enough. It would be better if we can check the current window size

Also, I think it would be nice to have the reset window size as a context manger such that after taking the screenshot, you will restore the window size.

So, what I have in mind is for the function `wait_to_compare_source_displayed_embed`, you can do:
```
with resize_window():
    display_embed_filepath = retry(get_embed_screenshot)(ctx)
```

Also, why do you have `retry` wrapped of `get_embed_screenshot`?

We applied some changes with @ddl-michael-noonan:
- Moved the retry to an intermediate function that is fully wrapped by the retry at the step definition.
- Removed the sleep as it was not necessary. It remained from when I was testing this out.
- Used the cucu config default browser size as a way to ensure the default viewport dimensions are used. The magic numbers are not there anymore and also it is not necessary to reset it back to the default as we are currently forcing the config's default.
Is this actually true of this function? Isn't this comment more about the example renders that you are source controlling to be compared against?

peep this comment -> https://github.com/cerebrotech/domino/pull/40067/files#r1520539651

But it genuinely seems like firefox-117 has nothing to do with this function in reality. I will have to have @ddl-galias walk me through this reasoning. Because I believe we just upgraded to 122 and it still worked.

The comment was wrongly placed by me. It should be at the step at line 86-89.

In the context of that other step, the reason why Fifefox is needed (we can remove the version) is that chromium headless wont render the embed, so you might try this in Chrome with UI and then it will fail either at the CI or in headless mode.

I moved the comment to the correct place and checked the wording with @ddl-michael-noonan and enhanced it too.
You are removing the system tests changes that @ddl-kgarton just made
